sn8.4:1.1: On one occasion Ven. Ānanda was staying near Sāvatthī in Jeta’s Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s monastery.
sn8.4:1.2: Then early in the morning, having adjusted his lower robe and taking his bowl & outer robe, he went into Sāvatthī for alms with Ven. Vaṅgīsa as his attendant monk.
sn8.4:1.3: Now at that time dissatisfaction (with the celibate life) had arisen in Ven. Vaṅgīsa. Lust invaded his mind.
sn8.4:1.4: So he addressed Ven. Ānanda with this verse:
sn8.4:2.4: tell me how to put it out.”
sn8.4:3.3: Shun the theme of the beautiful
